deployment_question: How does the model perform at locations separated from the training region?
definition: Hold out spatially contiguous regions, optionally with exclusion buffers around test blocks, to reduce spatial autocorrelation leakage between train and test.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: forces a minimum geographic or environmental distance from train to test

implementation_recipe: Choose spatial blocks or folds based on coordinates and target prediction scale.
Optionally remove training samples inside a buffer around held-out blocks.
Report spatial distance and environmental feature overlap between train and test.
quality_gates: Use a block size matched to spatial autocorrelation length or deployment scale.
Check train/test label and covariate balance after buffering.
Compare random CV with spatial CV to quantify optimism.
failure_modes: Overly large buffers can starve the training set.
Blocks can confound geography with unobserved environmental gradients.
Coordinates may leak through spatially encoded predictors.

citations.title: Assessing the performance of spatial cross-validation approaches
citations.evidence: Finds that spatially contiguous assessment sets with exclusion buffers improve estimates for spatially structured data.

citations.title: Estimating the Prediction Performance of Spatial Models via Spatial k-Fold Cross Validation
citations.evidence: Shows spatial k-fold validation reduces optimistic bias caused by spatial autocorrelation.
